title: measurement-annotator
description: JEAB Measurement category. Declares when and how the effect of the Core contingency is read: session termination rules, steady-state criteria, and baseline measurement conditions.

session_end.description: Session termination rule. Declares when a session ends.

errors.SESSION_END_MISSING_PARAM: rule=first requires both time and reinforcers
errors.SESSION_END_INVALID_COMBO: rule=time_only forbids reinforcers; rule=reinforcers_only forbids time
errors.SESSION_END_NONPOSITIVE_REINFORCERS: reinforcers must be a positive integer

session_end.reference: Baron, A., & Perone, M. (1998). Experimental design and analysis in the laboratory study of human operant behavior. In K. A. Lattal & M. Perone (Eds.), Handbook of research methods in human operant behavior (pp. 45-91). Plenum.

baseline.description: Baseline measurement declaration. Captures the operant level of the target response before experimental training.

pre_training_sessions.description: Number of sessions allocated to baseline measurement

errors.BASELINE_INVALID_SESSIONS: pre_training_sessions must be a positive integer

baseline.reference: Skinner, B. F. (1938). The behavior of organisms. Appleton-Century-Crofts.

steady_state.description: Steady-state criterion for phase termination. Declares when responding is considered stable enough to end a phase.

window_sessions.description: Number of recent sessions evaluated for stability

max_change_pct.description: Maximum percent change in measure across the window (e.g., 10 means ±10%)

min_sessions.description: Minimum total sessions in the phase before stability check applies

steady_state.reference: Sidman, M. (1960). Tactics of scientific research: Evaluating experimental data in psychology. Basic Books.

dependent_measure.description: Primary dependent variable declaration. Specifies which behavioral measures are recorded, reported, and analyzed. Corresponds to the 'Dependent Variable' subsection of JEAB Method / Measurement.

variables.description: List of dependent variables to record. 'rate' = responses per unit time; 'irt' = inter-response time distribution; 'prf_pause' = post-reinforcement pause duration; 'latency' = response latency from discriminative stimulus onset; 'bout_rate' = bout initiation rate (Shull et al., 2001); 'bout_length' = mean responses per bout; 'within_bout_rate' = response rate within bouts; 'running_rate' = response rate excluding post-reinforcement pauses; 'reinforcers' = reinforcer deliveries per session; 'consumption' = amount consumed per session (for devaluation studies); 'response_rate_ratio' = ratio of response rates across conditions (for devaluation / transfer tests); 'peak_time' = modal response time in peak procedure / FI scallop (Gibbon, 1977); 'spread' = width of temporal response distribution around peak; 'cv' = coefficient of variation of temporal discrimination (Weber fraction); 'breakpoint' = last completed ratio in progressive ratio (Hodos, 1961); 'demand_elasticity' = elasticity parameter from demand curve analysis (Hursh & Silberberg, 2008); 'suppression_ratio' = Estes-Skinner conditioned suppression ratio B/(A+B); 'changeover_rate' = rate of switching between concurrent schedule alternatives; 'custom' = user-defined measure

primary.description: Which variable is the primary dependent measure. Must be one of the values listed in 'variables'. Defaults to the first element of 'variables'.

errors.DEPENDENT_MEASURE_EMPTY_VARIABLES: variables must contain at least one measure
errors.DEPENDENT_MEASURE_PRIMARY_NOT_IN_VARIABL: primary must be one of the declared variables

dependent_measure.reference: Johnston, J. M., & Pennypacker, H. S. (2009). Strategies and tactics of behavioral research (3rd ed.). Routledge.

training_volume.description: Training volume tracking declaration. Specifies cumulative training exposure metrics to record across sessions. Critical for overtraining paradigms and habit formation research where the amount of training determines whether behavior is goal-directed or habitual (Adams & Dickinson, 1981).

track.description: Cumulative metrics to track across the training phase. 'sessions' = total session count; 'responses' = cumulative response count; 'reinforcers' = cumulative reinforcer deliveries

criterion_sessions.description: Session count threshold for overtraining classification. When cumulative sessions reach this value, session-analyzer flags the transition from moderate to extensive training.

criterion_responses.description: Cumulative response count threshold for overtraining classification

criterion_reinforcers.description: Cumulative reinforcer count threshold for overtraining classification

errors.TRAINING_VOLUME_EMPTY_TRACK: track must contain at least one metric
errors.TRAINING_VOLUME_NONPOSITIVE_CRITERION: criterion values must be positive integers

training_volume.reference: Adams, C. D., & Dickinson, A. (1981). Instrumental responding following reinforcer devaluation. Quarterly Journal of Experimental Psychology, 33B(2), 109-121. https://doi.org/10.1080/14640748108400816

microstructure.description: Response microstructure measurement parameters. Configures fine-grained temporal analysis of response patterns including IRT distributions, bout analysis, and post-reinforcement pauses. These parameters define how the session-analyzer decomposes raw event streams into structural units of behavior.

irt_bin_width.description: Bin width for IRT frequency distribution histograms (e.g., 0.5s). Determines the temporal resolution of IRT analysis. If omitted, session-analyzer uses adaptive binning.

burst_threshold.description: IRT threshold below which consecutive responses are classified as within-bout (burst). Responses with IRT < burst_threshold belong to the same bout. Typically determined via log-survivor analysis (Shull et al., 2001). If omitted, bout analysis is not performed.

prf_pause_method.description: Method for measuring post-reinforcement pause. 'latency_to_first' = time from reinforcer delivery to first response (Felton & Lyon, 1966); 'latency_to_nth' = time from reinforcer delivery to nth response (requires prf_pause_n); 'quarter_life' = time to emit 25% of ratio requirement (Herrnstein & Morse, 1957)

prf_pause_n.description: Number of responses for the latency_to_nth method (e.g., 3 = time to 3rd response post-reinforcement)

report.description: Which microstructure analyses to include in session reports. 'irt_distribution' = IRT frequency histogram; 'bout_statistics' = bout count, mean bout length, within-bout rate, bout initiation rate; 'prf_pause' = post-reinforcement pause durations per ratio; 'run_length' = response run lengths between pauses; 'changeover' = changeover delays and frequencies (for concurrent schedules); 'log_survivor' = IRT log-survivor plot for bout/pause threshold identification (Shull et al., 2001); 'scallop_index' = FI scallop curvature index (acceleration of responding within the interval); 'reinforcement_proximity' = IRT binning relative to reinforcement delivery (molecular analysis of reinforcement contiguity effects)

errors.MICROSTRUCTURE_NONPOSITIVE_BIN: irt_bin_width must be a positive time value
errors.MICROSTRUCTURE_NONPOSITIVE_THRESHOLD: burst_threshold must be a positive time value
errors.MICROSTRUCTURE_NTH_FORBIDDEN: prf_pause_n is only valid with prf_pause_method=latency_to_nth

microstructure.reference: Shull, R. L., Gaynor, S. T., & Grimes, J. A. (2001). Response rate viewed as engagement bouts: Effects of rate of reinforcement. Journal of the Experimental Analysis of Behavior, 75(3), 247-274. https://doi.org/10.1901/jeab.2001.75-247

phase_end.description: Phase termination criteria. Declares compound conditions for ending an experimental phase. Unlike @session_end (which governs individual sessions) and @steady_state (which evaluates stability), @phase_end specifies when the current experimental phase as a whole terminates — potentially requiring multiple criteria to converge simultaneously.

rule.description: How multiple criteria combine. 'all' = every criterion must be met (conjunction); 'any' = at least one criterion (disjunction); 'first' = whichever criterion is met first (identical to 'any' but signals intent)

min_sessions.description: Minimum number of sessions before phase can terminate

max_sessions.description: Maximum number of sessions (phase terminates regardless of other criteria)

stability.description: Reference to stability criterion. 'steady_state' = use the @steady_state parameters declared in the same program; 'custom' = custom stability evaluation (implementation-defined)

min_reinforcers.description: Minimum cumulative reinforcers before phase can terminate

cv_threshold.description: Maximum coefficient of variation of the primary DV across consecutive sessions (e.g., 0.15 = CV < 15%). Evaluated over the @steady_state window.

errors.PHASE_END_NO_CRITERIA: at least one criterion (min_sessions, max_sessions, stability, min_reinforcers, cv_threshold) must be specified
errors.PHASE_END_MISSING_STEADY_STATE: stability=steady_state requires @steady_state to be declared in the same program

phase_end.reference: Sidman, M. (1960). Tactics of scientific research: Evaluating experimental data in psychology. Basic Books.

logging.description: Event-level data recording specification. Declares what raw events and derived signals the runtime should write to the session log. This controls the granularity of data available for post-hoc analysis by session-analyzer.

events.description: Which event types to record. 'response' = individual response timestamps with sub-ms precision; 'reinforcer' = reinforcer delivery onset/offset; 'stimulus_onset'/'stimulus_offset' = discriminative/conditioned stimulus transitions; 'schedule_state' = schedule parameter changes (Adj step, Pctl threshold shift); 'timer_tick' = periodic time markers at clock resolution; 'phase_change' = phase transition markers; 'session_marker' = session start/end with metadata

format.description: Output format for the event log. 'jsonl' = JSON Lines (one event per line); 'csv' = comma-separated values; 'medpc' = Med-PC compatible format

precision.description: Timestamp precision. 'ms' = milliseconds; 'us' = microseconds; 'ns' = nanoseconds. Must be compatible with @clock resolution.

errors.LOGGING_EMPTY_EVENTS: events must contain at least one event type

logging.reference: Tatham, T. A., & Zurn, K. R. (1989). The MED-PC experimental apparatus programming system. Behavior Research Methods, Instruments, & Computers, 21(2), 294-299. https://doi.org/10.3758/BF03205598

iri_window.description: Inter-reinforcement interval (IRI) aggregation window specification. Declares how to bin and analyze the time between successive reinforcer deliveries. Critical for timing research where IRI serves as an implicit temporal cue distinct from the nominal schedule parameter.

bin_width.description: Bin width for IRI frequency distribution (e.g., 5s). If omitted, session-analyzer uses adaptive binning.

normalize.description: Normalization method for IRI distributions. 'none' = raw frequency counts; 'proportion' = relative frequency (sums to 1); 'rate' = responses per unit time within each IRI bin

track_preceding_iri.description: If true, record the preceding IRI as a covariate for each inter-reinforcement interval. Enables analysis of IRI-to-IRI sequential dependencies and their effect on responding.

report.description: Which IRI analyses to report. 'distribution' = full IRI frequency histogram; 'mean' = arithmetic mean IRI; 'median' = median IRI; 'cv' = coefficient of variation; 'sequential' = IRI(n) vs IRI(n-1) scatter plot data

errors.IRI_WINDOW_NONPOSITIVE_BIN: bin_width must be a positive time value

iri_window.reference: Catania, A. C., & Reynolds, G. S. (1968). A quantitative analysis of the responding maintained by interval schedules of reinforcement. Journal of the Experimental Analysis of Behavior, 11(S3), 327-383. https://doi.org/10.1901/jeab.1968.11-s327

warmup_exclude.description: Warmup exclusion declaration. Specifies an initial period of each session to exclude from dependent variable calculations. Standard practice in free-operant research to remove adaptation artifacts at session onset.

duration.description: Duration to exclude from the beginning of each session (e.g., 5min). Events during this period are still logged (if @logging is active) but excluded from DV calculations.

scope.description: What to exclude. 'analysis' = exclude from DV computation only (events still logged); 'logging_and_analysis' = exclude from both logging and analysis

errors.WARMUP_EXCLUDE_NONPOSITIVE: duration must be a positive time value
errors.WARMUP_EXCLUDE_EXCEEDS_SESSION: duration must be less than @session_end time (if both are declared)

warmup_exclude.reference: Ferster, C. B., & Skinner, B. F. (1957). Schedules of reinforcement. Appleton-Century-Crofts.

session.description: Trial-based session structure declaration (added by R-7 Phase 2). Specifies the structural/volumetric organization of trials per session — distinct from @session_end (which specifies termination rules). Used primarily for MTS / GoNoGo / DMTS and other trial-based procedures where session length is reported in JEAB Method sections as 'N trials per session' or 'K blocks of M trials'.

trials.description: Total number of trials per session. Required unless both `blocks` and `block_size` are given (in which case trials = blocks × block_size is computed).

blocks.description: Number of trial blocks per session. If given, `block_size` must also be given.

block_size.description: Trials per block. Arntzen (2012) reports that the modal block size in stimulus equivalence research is 18–36 trials.

errors.SESSION_MISSING_PARAM: at least one of `trials`, or (`blocks` and `block_size`), must be specified
errors.SESSION_BLOCKS_WITHOUT_BLOCK_SIZE: `blocks` was specified but `block_size` was not — both are required when declaring block structure
errors.SESSION_BLOCK_TRIALS_MISMATCH: when `trials`, `blocks`, and `block_size` are all given, trials must equal blocks × block_size
errors.SESSION_NONPOSITIVE_TRIALS: trials must be a positive integer
errors.SESSION_NONPOSITIVE_BLOCKS: blocks must be a positive integer
errors.SESSION_NONPOSITIVE_BLOCK_SIZE: block_size must be a positive integer

warnings.SESSION_OVERLAPS_SESSION_END: both @session and @session_end declared — runtime must reconcile trial-count vs time/reinforcer termination (OR semantics: whichever first)
warnings.SESSION_UNUSUAL_BLOCK_SIZE: block_size outside typical stimulus-equivalence research range (18–36; Arntzen 2012)

session.reference: Arntzen, E. (2012). Training and testing parameters in formation of stimulus equivalence: Methodological issues. European Journal of Behavior Analysis, 13(1), 123-135. https://doi.org/10.1080/15021149.2012.11434412

probe_policy.description: Probe/baseline trial-mixing policy for stimulus-equivalence testing and related paradigms (added by R-7 Phase 2). Strongly defines the semantics of probe trial interspersion with baseline-reinforced trials, the probe ratio, and the ordering regime. Supersedes the weakly-defined `@test_criterion.baseline_reinforced` (the latter is retained as an alias for backward compatibility).

baseline_reinforced.description: Whether baseline (trained) trials within the probe session continue to receive their training-phase consequence schedule. Fields et al. (1997) recommend true to prevent extinction artifacts during equivalence testing.

probe_ratio.description: Fraction of session trials that are probe trials. Typical value is 0.5 (50% probe / 50% baseline; Fields et al. 1997). 1.0 corresponds to all-probe (risk: extinction).

interspersed.description: If true, baseline and probe trials are mixed at the trial level (pseudo-random). If false, they are presented in blocks (separated).

order.description: Ordering regime for probe introduction. 'random' = trial-level random mixing; 'blocked' = probe block(s) after baseline block(s); 'progressive' = gradually increasing probe ratio across sessions (simple-to-complex protocol; Arntzen 2012).

errors.PROBE_POLICY_INCONSISTENT_INTERSPERSED_O: interspersed=true is incompatible with order='blocked' (blocked ordering implies non-interspersed)

warnings.PROBE_POLICY_EXTINCTION_RISK: baseline_reinforced=false with probe_ratio < 1.0 — baseline trials run in extinction, which may confound equivalence emergence measurement
warnings.PROBE_POLICY_ALL_PROBE: probe_ratio=1.0 — entire session runs in extinction (no training trials); acceptable for single-session probe testing but risky for longer sessions

probe_policy.reference: Fields, L., Reeve, K. F., Rosen, D., Varelas, A., Adams, B. J., Belanich, J., & Hobbie, S. A. (1997). Using the simultaneous protocol to study equivalence class formation: The facilitating effects of nodal number and size of previously established equivalence classes. Journal of the Experimental Analysis of Behavior, 67(3), 367-389. https://doi.org/10.1901/jeab.1997.67-367
